title: LL confirmatory runner (thin follow-up of run_osr_confirmatory) + section-7 label decision (pooled vs per-instrument)
source: pm-day 2026-06-12 (execution-engineer report on the Jun-18 confirmatory runner)
why: Jun-18 GO/NO-GO needs BOTH sleeves runnable without hand-written day-of code, and the prereg section-3 single-label vs per-instrument OSR reality is an unresolved amendment that could disqualify the gate if improvised on the day.
scope: (1) scripts/run_ll_confirmatory.py reusing run_osr_confirmatory module-level helpers (weekend_stratum, clean_sr_trials, resolve_label, refusal pattern) + LL data plumbing (two books, HY precondition, ll_events/ll_signal, 20-min timeout, minute bars); gate per prereg section-4 LL row (N>=50, PSR>=0.90, >=0 at 2x, DSR>0 at K=8). (2) DECISION before Jun-18: section-3 registers ONE label per sleeve but OSR runs per instrument - resolve pooled vs per-instrument (runner accepts prefix-enforced instrument-suffixed labels) and record in prereg section-7 amendment log. Methodology-load-bearing: surface to CEO with quant-phd-advisor input if contested.
